title: Measurement and report for cross-link interference management based on signal strength
abstract: A method in a user equipment (UE) (110B) is disclosed. The method comprises obtaining (404) an indication of one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE. The method comprises performing (408) one or more measurements on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. The method comprises reporting (412), to a network node (115), a measurement value for each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, the measurement value indicative of cross-link interference at the UE due to transmissions by one or more different UEs (110A, 110C, 110D, 110E).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method in a user equipment (UE) (HOB), comprising: obtaining (404) an indication of one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE; performing (408) one or more measurements on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources; and reporting (412), to a network node (115), a measurement value for each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, the measurement value indicative of cross-link interference at the UE due to transmissions by one or more different UEs (110A, HOC, HOD, 110E). 2. The method of Claim 1, wherein the one or more measurements are performed based on received energy. 3. The method of any of Claims 1-2, wherein the one or more measurements are performed without knowledge of any sequences associated with the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 4. The method of any of Claims 1-3, wherein performing one or more measurements on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises: performing a Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurement on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 5. The method of Claim 4, wherein the RSSI measurement comprises an average of total received power observed in a set of time and frequency resources. 6. The method of Claim 4, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a single-shot measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources in one slot; and the reported measurement value is a value of the single-shot measurement. 7. The method of Claim 4, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a set of measurements performed over a set of slots, each slot carrying one of the one or more time and frequency resource sets; and the reported measurement value comprises a set of measurements with one reported RSSI value per slot. 8. The method of Claim 4, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources that spans multiple slots; and the reported measurement value comprises an averaged measurement over the multiple slots. 9. The method of Claim 4, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources that occurs in a pre-configured set of slots; and the reported measurement value comprises a measured value for a slot in the pre- configured set of slots if the measured value satisfies a reporting trigger. 10. The method of any of Claims 1-9, wherein the obtained indication of one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE comprises one or more of: a sub-band index; a comb number corresponding to a set of time and frequency resources in a sub-band; a set of Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) symbols in a slot; and a set of slots. 11. The method of Claim 10, comprising deriving the one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE based on the obtained indication. 12. The method of any of Claims 1-11, further comprising: obtaining a configuration for a Sounding Reference Signal (SRS) transmission by the UE; and 

 transmitting the SRS according to the obtained configuration. 13. A method in a network node (115), comprising: configuring (504) a first user equipment (UE) (HOB) to perform one or more measurements on one or more sets of time and frequency resources; and receiving (508) a measurement value from the first UE for each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, the measurement value indicative of cross-link interference at the first UE due to transmissions by one or more different UEs (110A, HOC, HOD, 110E). 14. The method of Claim 13, further comprising: configuring the one or more different UEs to transmit a signal on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 15. The method of Claim 14, wherein the one or more different UEs are configured to transmit a Sounding Reference Signal (SRS). 16. The method of Claim 13, further comprising: receiving, from a neighboring network node, an SRS transmission configuration for the one or more different UEs, the SRS transmission configuration indicating that the one or more different UEs are configured to perform SRS transmissions on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, wherein: the first UE is configured to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources according to the received SRS transmission configuration. 17. The method of any of Claims 13-16, comprising: estimating, based on the received measurement value, an amount of cross-link interference at the first UE due to transmissions by the one or more different UEs. 18. The method of Claim 17, comprising: scheduling the one or more different UEs such that the amount of cross-link interference at the first UE is reduced. 19. The method of any of Claims 13-18, wherein configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises: signaling an indication of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the first UE. 20. The method of Claim 19, wherein the indication comprises one or more of: a sub-band index; a comb number corresponding to a set of time and frequency resources in a sub-band; a set of Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) symbols in a slot; and a set of slots. 21. The method of any of Claims 13-20, wherein configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises: configuring the first UE to perform the one or more measurements based on received energy. 22. The method of any of Claims 13-21, wherein configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises: configuring the first UE to perform the one or more measurements without knowledge of any sequences associated with the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 23. The method of any of Claims 13-22, wherein configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises: configuring the first UE to perform a Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurement on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 24. The method of Claim 23, wherein the RSSI measurement comprises an average of total received power observed in a set of time and frequency resources. 25. The method of any of Claims 13-24, wherein: configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises configuring the first UE to perform a set of measurements over a set of slots, each slot carrying one of the one or more time and frequency resource sets; and the received measurement value comprises a set of measurements with one reported RSSI value per slot. 26. The method of any of Claims 13-24, wherein: configuring the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises configuring the first UE to perform a measurement over a set of time and frequency resources that spans multiple slots; and the received measurement value comprises an averaged measurement over the multiple slots. 27. A user equipment (UE) (HOB), comprising: a receiver (610); a transmitter (610); and processing circuitry (620) coupled to the receiver and the transmitter, the processing circuitry configured to: obtain (404) an indication of one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE; perform (408) one or more measurements on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources; and report (412), to a network node (115), a measurement value for each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, the measurement value indicative of cross-link interference at the UE due to transmissions by one or more different UEs (110A, HOC, HOD, 110E). 28. The UE of Claim 27, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to perform the one or more measurements based on received energy. 29. The UE of any of Claims 27-28, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to perform the one or more measurements without knowledge of any sequences associated with the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 30. The UE of any of Claims 27-29, wherein the processing circuitry configured to perform one or more measurements on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises processing circuitry configured to: perform a Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) measurement on each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 31. The UE of Claim 30, wherein the RSSI measurement comprises an average of total received power observed in a set of time and frequency resources. 32. The UE of Claim 30, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a single-shot measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources in one slot; and the reported measurement value is a value of the single-shot measurement. 33. The UE of Claim 30, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a set of measurements performed over a set of slots, each slot carrying one of the one or more time and frequency resource sets; and the reported measurement value comprises a set of measurements with one reported RSSI value per slot. 34. The UE of Claim 30, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources that spans multiple slots; and the reported measurement value comprises an averaged measurement over the multiple slots. 35. The UE of Claim 30, wherein: the RSSI measurement comprises a measurement performed over a set of time and frequency resources that occurs in a pre-configured set of slots; and 

 the reported measurement value comprises a measured value for a slot in the pre- configured set of slots if the measured value satisfies a reporting trigger. 36. The UE of any of Claims 27-35, wherein the obtained indication of one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE comprises one or more of: a sub-band index; a comb number corresponding to a set of time and frequency resources in a sub-band; a set of Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) symbols in a slot; and a set of slots. 37. The UE of Claim 36, wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to derive the one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the UE based on the obtained indication. 38. The UE of any of Claims 27-37, wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to: obtain a configuration for a Sounding Reference Signal (SRS) transmission by the UE; and transmit the SRS according to the obtained configuration. 39. A network node (115), comprising: a receiver (710); a transmitter (710); and processing circuitry (720) coupled to the receiver and the transmitter, the processing circuitry configured to: configure (504) a first user equipment (UE) (HOB) to perform one or more measurements on one or more sets of time and frequency resources; and receive (508) a measurement value from the first UE for each of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources, the measurement value indicative of cross-link interference at the first UE due to transmissions by one or more different UEs (110A, HOC, HOD, 110E). 40. The network node of Claim 43, wherein the processing circuitry is further configured to schedule the one or more different UEs such that the amount of cross-link interference at the first UE is reduced. 45. The network node of any of Claims 39-44, wherein the processing circuitry configured to configure the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises processing circuitry configured to: signal an indication of the one or more sets of time and frequency resources to be measured by the first UE. 46. The network node of Claim 45, wherein the indication comprises one or more of: a sub-band index; a comb number corresponding to a set of time and frequency resources in a sub-band; a set of Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) symbols in a slot; and a set of slots. 47. The network node of any of Claims 39-46, wherein the processing circuitry configured to configure the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises processing circuitry configured to: configure the first UE to perform the one or more measurements based on received energy. 48. The network node of any of Claims 39-47, wherein the processing circuitry configured to configure the first UE to perform one or more measurements on the one or more sets of time and frequency resources comprises processing circuitry configured to: configure the first UE to perform the one or more measurements without knowledge of any sequences associated with the one or more sets of time and frequency resources. 49.
